place: Allegany village, New York, New York

facts.text: Allegany’s historic church, built in 1855 by the Methodist Episcopal Congregation, remains a landmark in the village.
The town’s name may come from the Allegewi tribe that once lived along the Allegheny River or from a Native word meaning "beautiful."
Allegany was first settled around 1820 by Ebenezer Reed, a Connecticut native, before becoming the Town of Burton in 1831.
The Allegany Area Historical Association, founded during the village’s 1981 sesquicentennial, preserves and shares the region’s history.
The former United Methodist Church at 25 N. serves as the AAHA Heritage Center, housing the town’s historical artifacts.
